title: 4 Requesting or Modifying ORS Service
body_text:
4	Requesting or Modifying ORS Service
4.1	Overview of Requesting or Modifying ORS Service
During enrollment to ORS, your financial institution determines if it will use ORS to enter cheque returns, AFT returns, and AFT Notice of Change transactions directly on ORS. Alternatively, financial institutions can upload its cheque return files, AFT return files, and AFT Notice of Change files to ORS manually or via Central 1âs FTP Server. See below for a list of ORS enrollment options.
Online Cheque Returns
Financial Institutions that enroll in this option have access to the following ORS functions:
â
Cheque returns
â
Cheque search (view and validate Incoming Clearing Items)
â
Deposit research
â
Reinstate cheques (correct cheque details)
â
Identification of suspect items (view cheques that are possible duplicate payments)
Online AFT Returns
Financial institutions that enroll in this option have access the following ORS features:
â
AFT returns
â
AFT search
â
AFT Notice of Change (create NOCs)
Upload cheque return files manually or via Central 1âs FTP server
Upload AFT return files manually or via Central 1âs FTP server
Upload AFT Notice of Change files manually or via Central 1âs FTP server
4.2	Requesting or Modifying ORS Service
To set up or modify ORS service for your financial institution, complete the Online Return System (ORS) Application, Form 2193, and forward it as directed on the form. Completion of the form is self-explanatory. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Online Return System (ORS) Application, Form 2193
Central 1 completes the Central 1 Use Only section of the form and returns it under confidential cover to your financial institution. If applicable, the script password is communicated to your financial institution contact person separately.
Related Information
Online Return System (ORS) Application,
Form 2193
END
